send_to_cs: No ESC for this client, send everything to CS
Send to CS for refund inquiries
tone: Close the convo once the client takes over
Close the convo if the cx first message is ok, no, etc.
Don't use the word "off" when talking about promotions
dos_and_donts: Don't proactively offer to find pricing info, redirect them to the website instead
promo_and_exclusions: Do not offer a coupon to first time purchasers/subscribers. There is not an active discount code at this time. Use DCI shortcut

Any questions I can answer for you?
What's the best mattress for young kids
4 year olds first bed

Nectar: For a young child transitioning to their first bed, I'd recommend a Twin size mattress. It's a great size for kids and it offers plenty of room for them to grow into. Our Nectar Memory Foam mattress could be a good option. It provides excellent support and comfort for all types of sleepers. You can check it out here: https://nectarsleep.attn.tv/sl/QjOPZGa6
My wife and I have the copper and love it but that's too fancy for our kid

It's going to be his bed hopefully forever so we are between the nectar or the nectar premier and aren't sure if it's worth the upgrade because right now he can sleep on the floor with a blanket and be comfortable

Nectar: Both the Nectar Memory Foam Mattress and the Nectar Premier would be great choices. The Nectar Memory Foam Mattress is a classic and provides excellent support and comfort. The Nectar Premier offers a bit more luxury with an added layer of memory foam. If you're looking for a mattress that will last and continue to provide comfort as your child grows, either would be a great choice. 

Nectar Memory Foam Mattress: https://nectarsleep.attn.tv/sl/jG-ZcJUf
Nectar Premier: https://nectarsleep.attn.tv/sl/8diV97IV
Nectar: *VIP's, your Memorial Day Deal has arrived!* Up to 40% OFF on America's most awarded mattress starts NOW: https://nectarsleep.attn.tv/aVi24F3_SNu0 .

You constantly have a 40% off sale and I see there is a class action lawsuit against you for not actually selling your beds for the crossed through original price. Do you plan on having an actual sale for Memorial Day or just the 40% lie?

send_to_cs: No ESC for this client, send everything to CS using the "Escalation template"
templates: For 10% off welcome code issues use temp 'Follow up 1', then 'Follow up 2' after confirming the item is excluded from promos
For fridge recommendations use FRR shortcut
For service-related issues/scheduled repair service appointments use SRI shortcut
For cxs looking for replacement parts/accessories from an affiliate Parts Distributor use RPA shortcut
If a cx has an inquiry for finding the serial number/model number of an item:
➜ If it's a TV, use mnt shortcut
➜ If it's another product, use mna shortcut
dos_and_donts: If it's a new conversation and we didn't introduce ourselves in the automated message, start your message with: "This is Jordan at LG."

conversation.message_text: Backcountry: Reply Y to subscribe to recurring automated personalized marketing alerts (e.g. cart reminders) from Backcountry.
Backcountry: Welcome to Backcountry! Msg & data rates may apply. Msg frequency varies. Reply HELP for help, STOP to cancel. Privacy: attnl.tv/p/iVG

Backcountry: Welcome to the Herd! You gave us your digits, we'll give you some, too. Your code for 15% off full-price items is here: http://bctxt.co/acRkvKHPjTqa 
Exclusions apply.

Backcountry: Oh, also, a lot of people like getting personal recs from us. Over the next few days we’ll try to help you find something that you will love.

Some of our favorite gear just went on sale! Get the best deals on our top brands before they're gone: http://bctxt.co/aHL1OPik_ezC  .

Backcountry: Considering new hiking boots? I'm here to help. 

And no matter what, remember to use code BCNTFQ124JDRVSCW for 15% off full-price items!

Click here and use your offer: http://bctxt.co/aihXX-Yvo2r6 .
Yes I am backing in the Dolomites
Need good traction boots
Backcountry: Do you see anything you like in particular or would you like me to make a recommendation?
But prefer high boots
Please do
Backcountry: For high boots with good traction, I recommend the Salomon Quest 4 GTX. They offer excellent grip and ankle support, perfect for the Dolomites. Check them out here: backcountry.com
Right now I have a pair of ahnus very comfortable high with zipper but they don't make them  anymore
Backcountry: If you liked Ahnu, you might enjoy the Merrell Moab 2 Mid Waterproof Boots. They offer great comfort and traction. Check them out here: backcountry.com
Your links don't work
Backcountry: I apologize for the inconvenience. Please visit backcountry.com and search for "Merrell Moab 2 Mid Waterproof Boots" for more details.
I don't like Merrell

Backcountry: If Merrell isn't your preference, consider the Lowa Renegade GTX Mid Boots. They offer excellent comfort and traction. Visit backcountry.com and search for "Lowa Renegade GTX Mid Boots" for more details.
I prefer higher boots and only the ones made in Europe or USA

Backcountry: For high boots made in Europe or the USA, consider the Zamberlan Vioz GTX. They offer great traction and support. Visit backcountry.com and search for "Zamberlan Vioz GTX" for more details.
Aren't they ankle height
Looks low

Backcountry: For higher boots made in Europe or the USA, consider the Danner Mountain Light II. They offer excellent support and are made in the USA. Visit backcountry.com and search for "Danner Mountain Light II" for more details.
